steps.reveal: Quare = therefore (qua + re = by which thing)

hints.where: 'Where' is 'ubi' — 'quare' is an inferential conjunction meaning 'therefore'
hints.because: 'Because' is 'quia' or 'quod' — 'quare' draws a conclusion, not states a cause

hints.accusative: 'De' always takes the ablative, never the accusative
hints.genitive: 'De' governs the ablative — genitive is not used with 'de'
hints.dative: 'De' governs the ablative — dative is not used with 'de'

steps.reveal: summa salute = the supreme safety (abl., governed by 'de')

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'summa salus' — 'summa salute' is ablative
hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'summam salutem' — 'summa salute' is ablative
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'summae salutis' — 'summa salute' is ablative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension has genitive in -ae — 'salutis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension has genitive in -i — 'salutis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension has genitive in -ūs — 'salutis' ends in -is

hints.nominative: Nominative feminine would also be 'vestra,' but here it agrees with ablative 'salute'
hints.accusative: Accusative feminine would be 'vestram' — 'vestra' agrees with ablative 'salute'
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'vestrae' — 'vestra' is ablative agreeing with 'salute'

hints.to perceive: 'To perceive' is 'cernere' — 'decernere' adds de- meaning to decide definitively
hints.to run: 'To run' is 'currere' — 'decernere' means to decide or decree

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'decernitis' — 'decernite' without the -is ending is imperative
hints.present subjunctive: Present subjunctive would be 'decernatis' — 'decernite' is imperative
hints.future indicative: Future would be 'decernetis' — 'decernite' is present imperative

hints.cerno, cernere, crevi, cretum: These are the parts of 'cernere' (to perceive) — the base verb without de-
hints.decerto, decertare, decertavi, decertatu: These are the parts of 'decertare' (to fight it out) — different verb
hints.discerno, discernere, discrevi, discretu: These are the parts of 'discernere' (to distinguish) — different prefix

steps.prompt: How does 'de summa salute vestra' differ from a simple objective genitive?
steps.target: 'de' + ablative expresses the topic of deliberation: 'concerning your supreme safety'
choices: 'de' + ablative expresses the topic of deliberation: 'concerning your supreme safety'
it expresses cause: 'because of your safety'
it expresses separation: 'away from your safety'

hints.it expresses cause: 'because of your saf: 'De' can indicate cause, but with 'decernere' it means 'concerning' — the topic of the decree
hints.it expresses separation: 'away from your: 'De' can mean 'from,' but with verbs of deciding it means 'concerning'
hints.it expresses time: 'during your safety': 'De' does not express time here — it indicates the subject matter of the decision

translation: Therefore decide carefully concerning your supreme safety
